metadata.title: International alignment matrix — overlapping/rival bloc memberships and the multi-alignment (hedging) trend (documented, dated)
metadata.status: bloc memberships are FACT (treaty/charter/membership records); the synthesis is a STRUCTURAL trend-conclusion (multi-alignment/hedging is the norm) stated WITHOUT imputing motive or a unitary bloc 'mind' (composition/division-fallacy guard)
metadata.frame: The synthesis block the user asked for: a matrix of who belongs to which bloc, surfacing the INCONSISTENCIES — the same states sit in rival blocs at once. Blocs: BRICS+, the Quad, AUKUS, NATO, the SCO, OPEC/OPEC+, the G7, the G20, the GCC, ASEAN, the African Union, and the competing corridors (#143). The trend (documented, not psychologized): bloc membership is NON-EXCLUSIVE and hedged — India is the archetype (BRICS + SCO + Quad + G20 + IMEC + INSTC), Turkey straddles NATO + the Middle Corridor + a BRICS application, and the Gulf states sit in OPEC + BRICS + US partnerships + IMEC. Pairs with #142 BRICS, #143 corridors, #146 OPEC. Attaches to the existing bloc + state nodes.

the_blocs: The overlapping clubs: BRICS+ (Brazil/Russia/India/China/South Africa + Egypt/Ethiopia/Iran/UAE/Indonesia...); the QUAD (US/Japan/India/Australia); AUKUS (US/UK/Australia); NATO (incl. the US/UK/Turkey); the SCO (China/Russia/India/Pakistan/Iran/Central Asia); OPEC/OPEC+; the G7 and the broader G20; the GCC (Gulf monarchies); ASEAN; the African Union; plus the rival infrastructure corridors. grade: fact (memberships).
the_inconsistencies: Documented multi-alignment (the 'inconsistencies'), stated as membership not motive: INDIA — BRICS + SCO + Quad + G20 + IMEC (with the US/Gulf/EU/Israel) + INSTC (with Russia/Iran) — the archetypal hedger. TURKEY — a NATO member that anchors the Russia-bypassing Middle Corridor, buys Russian energy/S-400s, and APPLIED to BRICS (2024). SAUDI ARABIA + UAE — OPEC + BRICS+ + US security partners + IMEC corridor sponsors. EGYPT/ETHIOPIA — BRICS + heavy US/IMF dependence. BRAZIL — BRICS + deep Western trade + the G20. So the same actors appear on rival sides of the same contests. grade: fact (overlapping memberships).
the_discipline: Per the composition/division-fallacy guard: a state's simultaneous membership in rival blocs is recorded as INSTITUTIONAL PARTICIPATION, not a feeling, a master plan, or a unitary national 'mind'; and a bloc's 'position' is not a single intention. The only defensible synthesis is STRUCTURAL: in a multipolar system, alignment is non-exclusive and HEDGED, which makes binary 'West vs BRICS / democracies vs autocracies' framings leaky descriptions of who actually cooperates with whom on what. grade: trend stated structurally (overlay).
the_honest_reading: The alignment 'matrix' shows that the headline blocs overlap heavily: the same swing states (India above all, then Turkey, the Gulf, Egypt, Brazil) belong to rival clubs simultaneously and hedge across them. The memberships are fact; the warranted conclusion is the structural one — multi-alignment/hedging is the norm, so bloc labels describe overlapping, leaky coalitions rather than fixed teams. No motive or unitary 'mind' is imputed to any state or bloc. This is the synthesis layer over #142 (BRICS), #143 (corridors), and #146 (OPEC). Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
